messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge. Use the full provided multi-section context to understand cross-references and repeated logic, then extract concise, high-value structured knowledge from the requested sections. Treat this as an industry standard/guideline source. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer actionable control logic: enable/disable logic, staging rules, trim-and-respond reset rules, alarm/fault equations, setpoints/timers/ranges, and commissioning or TAB checks. Do not extract front matter, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or ungrounded paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears in multiple sections, return the clearest canonical item once per applicable section. evidence_quote MUST be a SHORT verbatim contiguous substring copied from the section text. Do not use ellipses or combine non-contiguous bullets. Never include facts in summary, trigger_condition, or required_behavior unless they are supported by evidence_quote. If a requirement has multiple alternatives (for example option a and option b), split them into separate candidates, one candidate per alternative. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason.
[redacted: ASHRAE section text omitted from artifact]

      "title": "Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints (ASHRAE 62.1)",
      "section_id": "5.2.1.3",
      "section_title": "Generic Ventilation Zones",
      "equipment_scope": "VAV terminal units, fan-powered terminal units, SZVAV AHUs",
      "summary": "Calculates Vmin and Vmin* based on ventilation requirements, with modifications for occupancy, windows, and CO2 sensors.",
      "trigger_condition": "Zone requires mechanical ventilation and project complies with ASHRAE Standard 62.1.",
      "required_behavior": "Calculate Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Set Vmin = Voz if AHU has been 100% OA for 10 min, else 1.5*Voz if 'AUTO', else as entered. Set Vmin* = Vmin except as modified by occupancy/window/CO2 conditions. For CO2 DCV, use P-only loop to reset Vmin* and Vbz-P* in parallel.",
      "configurable_values": ["EzC (default 1.0)", "EzH (default 0.8)", "CO2 setpoint", "Vmin (if not AUTO)", "Vcool-max", "Vbz-P", "Vbz-A"],
      "fault_condition": null,
      "commissioning_check": null,
      "evidence_quote": "Vmin shall be equal to Voz as calculated in Section 5.2.1.3.f below if Vmin in Section 3.1.2 is “AUTO” and the associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (outdoor air damper fully open; return air damper fully closed) for 10 minutes;",
      "page_hint": 59,
      "confidence": 0.95
    },
    {

      "section_title": "Generic Ventilation Zones",
      "equipment_scope": "VAV terminal units, fan-powered terminal units, SZVAV AHUs",

Set Vmin* = Vmin except modified by occupancy/window/CO2. For CO2 DCV, use P-only loop to reset Vmin* from Vmin to Vcool-max (or VCO2-max) at 0-50% output; 50-100% output used at system level.",

      "summary": "Pulse width modulates airflow setpoint when active setpoint is below controllable minimum to meet average ventilation requirements.",

      "required_behavior": "Calculate TAVratio = Vspt/Vm. Set total cycle time TCT = 15 min (adjustable). Open period OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio). Closed period CP = TCT - OP. During open, set Vspt* = Vm; during closed, set Vspt* = 0 and disable damper loop. Randomize initial open period using RNDM*OP.",

      "required_behavior": "Prevent heating setpoint > cooling setpoint - 0.5°C (1°F). Limit occupied cooling setpoint to 22-27°C (72-80°F) and heating to 18-22°C (65-72°F). For demand limit, increase cooling setpoint by 0.5/1/2°C (1/2/4°F) for levels 1/2/3; decrease heating similarly. For open window, set heating to 4°C (40°F) and cooling to 49°C (120°F). For occupancy sensor, after 5 min unpopulated, decrease heating by 0.5°C (1°F) and increase cooling by 0.5°C (1°F); restore after 1 min populated.",
